hawkish_phrases: risks surrounding the economic outlook...had become more balanced
there was a case for guarded optimism
caution was warranted not to overrate the impact of monetary policy measures
policy rates had reached their effective lower bound
downside risks to growth were seen to stem from the external environment
dovish_phrases: expanded APP purchases were intended to be carried out until the end of September 2016
monetary policy measures played an important role in supporting economic growth by lowering borrowing costs
there is still considerable slack in the economy
the full implementation of all the monetary policy measures that had been decided
steady hand and the firm implementation of the measures decided in January 2015
summary: The Governing Council held all rates unchanged (main refinancing at 0.05%, deposit at -0.20%) and unanimously reaffirmed its commitment to continue the expanded asset purchase programme until end-September 2016, emphasizing firm implementation of existing measures with no consideration of policy changes, while noting improved momentum and inflation stabilization but considerable remaining slack.
